description: Maximum number of file descriptors system-wide (max int64 — effectively unlimited)
Maximum PID value — raised to 4M for high process counts (training, containers)
Total shared memory pages — set to max for large model training
Maximum shared memory segment size — set to max for large memory allocations
Maximum number of shared memory segments
Don't auto-remove shared memory on last detach
Maximum socket listen backlog — doubled for high concurrency
Maximum network device backlog — 50x increase for burst traffic
Default socket receive buffer size (1MB, up from 208KB)
Maximum socket receive buffer (64MB — for large data transfers)
Default socket send buffer size (1MB)
Maximum socket send buffer (64MB — for large data transfers)
Maximum ancillary buffer size per socket (2MB, 100x increase)
Busy polling timeout in usec for socket reads (reduces latency)
Busy reading timeout in usec for socket reads (reduces latency)
Default queueing discipline — Fair Queuing for better throughput with BBR
Enable BPF JIT compiler for eBPF programs
RPS socket flow table entries — enables receive packet steering
Swappiness — extremely high (180) for zram/zswap with 503GB RAM. Favors swapping to compressed memory.
Dirty background write threshold (128MB absolute instead of ratio)
Dirty background ratio (disabled — using bytes instead)
Dirty write threshold (256MB absolute — forces more frequent flushes)
Dirty ratio (disabled — using bytes instead for predictable I/O)
Dirty page expiry time (15s, halved from default 30s)
Writeback thread wakeup interval (15s)
Maximum memory map areas per process — set to max for large model mmap loading
Minimum free memory reserved (5.03GB — prevents OOM during burst allocations)
Memory overcommit mode: 0=heuristic (default), 1=always, 2=never
Overcommit ratio percentage (only used when overcommit_memory=2)
VFS cache reclaim tendency (100 = fair reclaim)
Pages to read on swap-in (0 = single page, optimal for zram/SSD)
Watermark boosting (disabled — reduces fragmentation overhead)
Watermark scale factor (12.5x default — larger free memory buffers)
Proactive memory compaction level (0-100)
Laptop mode (2 = aggressive writeback batching for power saving)
Number of 2MB hugepages reserved (not set — using THP instead)
Memory reserved for root user (8MB)
Memory reserved for user processes (128MB)

System is tuned for: large memory allocations (max shm, max mmap), high network throughput (64MB buffers, busy polling, fq qdisc), SSD/zram-optimized swap (swappiness 180, page-cluster 0), predictable dirty writeback (absolute bytes vs ratios)
recommended_for_training: verify vm.max_map_count and kernel.pid_max are at max for training workloads
